title: Xtra Light Argentinian Rice Casserole
description: A rich and light Argentinian dish featuring rice and crunchy elements, perfect for a hangover cure, and friendly to Buddhist temple dietary preferences. This casserole is encrouted with a crispy crust and features Rice Krispies for added crunch.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat the olive oil and saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked rice, crushed Rice Krispies, and shredded cheese.
Add the cooked mushroom mixture to the bowl and stir to combine.
Roll out the puff pastry on a floured surface to a large rectangle.
Spoon the rice mixture onto one half of the pastry, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Fold the other half of the pastry over the filling and press the edges to seal.
Brush the pastry with a little bit of water and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Place the casserole on a baking sheet and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
